rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving type errors in binary expressions and named things before adding new architecture.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when diagnostics reference subcomponent paths or outputs.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope and declared in the same annex.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type mismatches, trace the root cause to unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations and correct them.
Validate that all AGREE expressions use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed model's dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes should contain assumptions and guarantees referencing visible ports.
Implementation annexes should contain assignments and bindings for subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture to resolve AGREE type errors; instead, fix the root cause of the type mismatch.
Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-level paths in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes for implementation-level bindings
